heading: Public Lands Corps program
label: 1723 Public Lands Corps program
notes: References in TextThis subchapter, referred to in subsec. (a)(2), was in the original “this Act”, and was translated as reading “this title”, meaning title II of , known as the Public Lands Corps Act of 1993, to reflect the probable intent of Congress.Pub. L. 91–378

children.heading: Establishment of Public Lands Corps
children.label: a Establishment of Public Lands Corps

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: There is established in the Department of the Interior, the Department of Agriculture, and the Department of Commerce a corps, to be known as the “Public Lands Corps”.

children.heading: No effect on other agencies
children.label: 2 No effect on other agencies
children.text: Nothing in this subsection precludes the establishment of a public lands corps by the head of a Federal department or agency other than a department described in paragraph (1), in accordance with this subchapter.

children.heading: Participants
children.label: b Participants
children.text: The Corps shall consist of individuals between the ages of 16 and 30, inclusive, and veterans age 35 or younger who are enrolled as participants in the Corps by the Secretary. To be eligible for enrollment in the Corps, an individual shall satisfy the criteria specified in paragraphs (1), (2), (4), and (5) of . The Secretary may enroll such individuals in the Corps without regard to the civil service and classification laws, rules, or regulations of the United States. The Secretary may establish a preference for the enrollment in the Corps of individuals who are economically, physically, or educationally disadvantaged.section 12591(a) of title 42

children.heading: Qualified youth or conservation corps
children.label: c Qualified youth or conservation corps

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary is authorized to enter into contracts and cooperative agreements with any qualified youth or conservation corps to perform appropriate conservation projects referred to in subsection (d).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: For purposes of entering into contracts and cooperative agreements under paragraph (1), the Secretary may give preference to qualified youth or conservation corps located in a specific area that have a substantial portion of members who are economically, physically, or educationally disadvantaged to carry out projects within the area.

children.heading: Priority projects
children.label: B Priority projects
children.text: In carrying out priority projects in a specific area, the Secretary shall, to the maximum extent practicable, give preference to qualified youth or conservation corps located in that specific area that have a substantial portion of members who are economically, physically, or educationally disadvantaged.

children.heading: Projects to be carried out
children.label: d Projects to be carried out

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary may utilize the Corps or any qualified youth or conservation corps to carry out appropriate conservation projects which the Secretary is authorized to carry out under other authority of law on public lands.

children.heading: Projects on Indian lands
children.label: 2 Projects on Indian lands
children.text: Appropriate conservation projects may also be carried out under this subchapter on Indian lands with the approval of the Indian tribe involved and on Hawaiian home lands with the approval of the Department of Hawaiian Home Lands of the State of Hawaii.

children.heading: Disaster prevention or relief projects
children.label: 3 Disaster prevention or relief projects
children.text: The Secretary may authorize appropriate conservation projects and other appropriate projects to be carried out on Federal, State, local, or private land as part of a Federal disaster prevention or relief effort.
will provide long-term benefits to the public;
will instill in the enrollee involved a work ethic and a sense of public service;
can be planned and initiated promptly; and
will provide academic, experiential, or environmental education opportunities.

children.heading: Consistency
children.label: f Consistency
children.text: Each appropriate conservation project carried out under this subchapter on eligible service lands shall be consistent with the provisions of law and policies relating to the management and administration of such lands, with all other applicable provisions of law, and with all management, operational, and other plans and documents which govern the administration of the area.

children.heading: Effect
children.label: g Effect
children.text: Nothing in this section authorizes the use of the Public Lands Corps for projects on or impacting real property owned by, operated by, or within the custody, control, or administrative jurisdiction of the Administrator of General Services without the express permission of the Administrator of General Services.
